headline: Tech rebounds after AI-driven chip selloff
detail: Chip and AI names got hammered in a global selloff and are bouncing today. For your book - AVGO, SMH, CRWD, PLTR - the rebound is welcome but the underlying concern about AI spending sustainability hasn't gone away. A dead-cat bounce on margin is still dangerous if it rolls back over.

headline: Congress bill could make AI firms pay their own energy costs
detail: A bill moving in Congress would shift data center energy costs onto tech companies instead of ratepayers. This directly pressures margins for AI infrastructure plays, which is the core thesis behind AVGO and PLTR. If it gains traction, it's a valuation headwind for the names you're most concentrated in.

headline: Semiconductor stocks: history of one-day plunges
detail: A historical look at 17 prior one-day chip plunges over 15 years is relevant context for what just hit your SMH and AVGO positions. Historically these plunges have had mixed follow-throughs - not automatic recoveries. Useful framing as you decide whether to trim AVGO into any bounce.

headline: Brent crude hits multi-month low
detail: Oil falling below $76 signals softer global growth expectations, which can bleed into risk appetite broadly. Not a direct hit on your tech names, but weak macro sentiment makes it harder for beaten-down growth stocks like PLTR to recover quickly.
